Content: The vehicle shall provide automated lane centering assistance on highway-type roads so that the driver is not required to provide continuous steering correction under normal driving conditions.
Rationale: OEM product requirement to reduce driver workload and fatigue on highway drives.

Content: The driver shall be able to override or disengage the lane keeping assistance at any time by applying steering torque, consistent with regulatory requirements for driver authority over automated steering systems (e.g., UN R79).
Rationale: Regulatory requirement preserving driver authority over automated steering.

Content: Any malfunction of the lane keeping assistance function shall result in a safe, predictable vehicle state with no unintended or hazardous steering torque applied to the vehicle.
Rationale: Functional safety goal (ISO 26262) to avoid unintended actuation hazard.

Content: The driver shall be provided with clear and timely visual and/or audible indication of the current lane keeping assistance status (off, standby, active, driver override, or fault).
Rationale: HMI / customer requirement for situational awareness.

Content: The lane keeping assistance function shall only be available within a defined vehicle speed range appropriate for its intended highway driving use case.
Rationale: Product scope and safety requirement avoiding activation outside validated operating conditions.

Content: The system shall record diagnostic information for all detected malfunctions to support field diagnosis and maintenance by authorized service personnel.
The lane keeping assistance function shall disengage automatically when the driver indicates an intentional lane change maneuver.
Rationale: Customer/usability requirement to avoid fighting an intentional driver maneuver.

Content: The system shall detect loss, corruption, or implausible values of any safety-relevant input signal and transition to a safe state within a bounded reaction time.
Rationale: Functional safety requirement for bounded fault reaction per ISO 26262.

Content: The lane keeping assistance function shall maintain stable, non-oscillatory steering behavior under degraded lane-marking visibility conditions (e.g., rain, fading markings, glare).
Rationale: OEM robustness/product-quality requirement across real-world driving conditions.

Content: The steering torque control function of the lane keeping assistance system shall be developed, verified, and validated to ASIL D in accordance with ISO 26262, given its potential to directly influence vehicle lateral control.
Rationale: Functional safety classification requirement (ISO 26262 Part 3 HARA outcome).
